title: rosa canina subvar. insubrica
intro: Rosa canina subvar. insubrica, commonly known as the Italian or Insular Rose, is a subvariety of the Rosa canina, a species of rose native to Europe and Western Asia. This shrub-like perennial plant is valued for its fragrant flowers, edible hips (fruits), and medicinal properties. The health benefits of Rosa canina subvar. insubrica include its use in treating digestive issues, high blood pressure, and inflammation, thanks to its high content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. The therapeutic actions of this plant are attributed to its bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties.
benefits: Rosa canina subvar. insubrica has many benefits, such as its ability to provide a rich source of vitamins and antioxidants, which can help to boost the immune system and protect against oxidative stress. The plant's high content of vitamin C and E can also aid in the reduction of inflammation, improve heart health, and even help to combat certain types of cancer. Additionally, the Rosa canina subvar. insubrica plant has been known to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various skin conditions, such as acne, eczema, and wounds. By incorporating this plant into one's daily routine, individuals can experience improved overall health, increased energy levels, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases, ultimately leading to a longer and healthier life.
actions: Rosa canina subvar. insubrica has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to soothe and protect the skin, reduce pain and inflammation, and prevent infections. These properties can improve people's daily lives by providing relief from conditions like eczema, acne, and wounds, allowing individuals to engage in physical activities without discomfort or risk of infection. Additionally, the plant's antiviral and antifungal properties can help prevent the spread of illnesses, giving people more confidence in their daily interactions and work. By utilizing the therapeutic actions of Rosa canina subvar. insubrica, individuals can experience improved overall health and well-being, enhancing their quality of life and ability to participate in daily activities.
intro_study: According to "Iranian journal of pharmaceutical research : IJPR", rosa canina subvar. insubrica benefits from being a rich source of antioxidants and potential xanthine oxidase inhibitors, which can help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress.
constituents: Rosa canina subvar. insubrica has many active constituents, such as flavonoids (kaempferol and quercetin), phenolic acids (gallic acid and ellagic acid), and essential oils (geraniol and linalool), which contribute to its medicinal properties. These constituents have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, making the plant useful for wound healing, soothing skin irritations, and preventing infections. The flavonoids in Rosa canina subvar. insubrica, in particular, have been shown to improve cardiovascular health by reducing blood pressure and cholesterol levels, while the phenolic acids have been found to have neuroprotective effects, potentially preventing or slowing the progression of neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's. Overall, the medicinal constituents of Rosa canina subvar. insubrica can improve people's daily lives by providing natural remedies for various health issues, promoting overall well-being, and enhancing the quality of life.
parts: Rosa canina subvar. insubrica has many medicinal parts, such as flowers, hips (the fruit), leaves, and roots. The flowers and hips of the plant contain flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, as well as phenolic acids like gallic acid, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The leaves of Rosa canina subvar. insubrica contain saponins, such as rosarin and rosavin, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects. The roots of the plant contain tannins and phenolic compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
preparations: Rosa canina subvar. insubrica has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used for their medicinal properties. Rosa canina subvar. insubrica tea is used to treat digestive issues and as a mild laxative, while the tincture is used to treat menstrual cramps and inflammation. Rosa canina subvar. insubrica infusions are used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to their expectorant properties. Additionally, Rosa canina subvar. insubrica extracts are used in topical applications to treat skin irritations and wounds.
side_effects: Rosa canina subvar. insubrica can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, or interacting negatively with certain medications. Some users have reported experiencing diarrhea, stomach cramps, and nausea after consuming large quantities of the plant's extracts. In rare cases, improper use of Rosa canina subvar. insubrica can also lead to respiratory issues, including difficulty breathing and asthma-like symptoms. Additionally, pregnant or breastfeeding women are advised to avoid using the plant due to potential risks of birth defects or adverse effects on their baby's health.
